message.content:
Search across all 6 repos under "C:\repos\dflow plm" (designflow-backend, designflow-bff, designflow-data-syncing, designflow-frontend, designflow-item-master, designflow-tracking) for any documentation that explains why an aws-sdk file was deleted or removed. 

Look in:
- CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, HANDOFF.md, README.md, and any files under docs/ or .claude/
- Any markdown files that mention "aws-sdk", "aws sdk", "S3", "AWS" in the context of removal, deletion, deprecation, or migration away from it

Report the file paths and the relevant excerpts. Search breadth: thorough.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files) or Word templates (.dotx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', '.dotx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx or .dotx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review
- security-review

1	# AGENTS.md — Designflow Backend: Primary Developer Guide
2	
3	Read this file in full before touching any code. It is the authoritative orientation document for any developer or AI session working on this repository.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 1. What this service is
8	
9	Designflow Backend is the main REST API for the Designflow PLM platform. It is a stateless Node.js + Express service backed by PostgreSQL (Sequelize ORM). Identity comes from a JWT on every request; session state does not exist. The service runs on Google Cloud Run and is reached exclusively through the BFF (`designflow-bff`), which terminates the user JWT and adds an OIDC service-to-service token before forwarding. The frontend is a separate Angular SPA in a sibling repository. For a full system diagram see `docs/architecture.md`.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## 2. Multi-model AI note
14	
15	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
16	
17	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
18	
19	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
20	
21	---
22	
23	## 2a. Documentation map: what to read for each task
24	
25	Always start with this file (`AGENTS.md`). Then load other docs **only when relevant** — do
26	not ingest every `.md`.
27	
28	Cross-repo infrastructure standard: Designflow's current non-code runtime and
29	operating environment is tracked in
30	[u2giants/albert-standards `infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md`](https://github.com/u2giants/albert-standards/blob/main/infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md).
31	If a change affects Cloud Run topology, Cloud Build/deploy behavior, domains,
32	BFF routing, secrets/env handling, storage, auth/OIDC, or database operating
33	rules, update that standards page in the same docs pass.
34	
35	| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
36	|---|---|---|
37	| Quick orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | deep `docs/` |
38	| Modify endpoints / model / domain logic | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md` | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy changes |
39	| Add/change config, env vars, secrets, app settings | `AGENTS.md` (§12), `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if prod runtime affected | unrelated architecture docs |
40	| DB schema / migration / new column / data flow | `AGENTS.md` (§6 Decision tree, §8 Data model), `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/configuration.md` if env affected | `docs/deployment.md` unless rollout changes |
41	| Local setup, run, test, debug | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md` | `docs/deployment.md` |
42	| Deploy / Docker / Cloud Build / Cloud Run / rollback | `AGENTS.md` (§13), `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md` | `docs/development.md` |
43	| AI proxy (OpenRouter / DeepSeek), tariffs (Section 301 / Trump 2025), HTS | `AGENTS.md` (§3, §11), `docs/architecture.md` | local-only dev docs |
44	| MCP server work | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/mcp-integration.md` | unrelated module docs |
45	| Internal REST endpoint reference | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/api-reference.md`, `routes/lib.router.js` | deployment docs |
46	| Investigate a bug/incident | `AGENTS.md` (§14 Critical incident log), the area's doc, `HANDOFF.md` if present | unrelated docs |
47	| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md` if present, docs it names | docs outside the handoff scope |
48	| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | other docs unless needed |
49	| Docs-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected `docs/` | source except to verify |
50	
51	No backend `HANDOFF.md` is currently required. Update this table whenever a doc is added, removed, or repurposed.
52	
53	---
54	
55	## 3. Repository structure
56	
57	| Path | What it is |
58	|---|---|
59	| `index.js` | Server entry — middleware stack (including the `x-user-authorization` → `authorization` swap) and `listen` |
60	| `config/env.js` | dotenv loader; resolves `.env.${NODE_ENV}` |
61	| `config/db.config.js` | PostgreSQL connection params; throws on missing required key |
62	| `config/digitalocean.config.js` | DigitalOcean Spaces credentials |
63	| `config/brevo.config.js` | Brevo email credentials |
64	| `helpers/auth.js` | JWT RS256 verify; `authRole(roles[])` middleware factory; `getUserInfo(token)` |
65	| `helpers/context.middleware.js` | `AsyncLocalStorage` per-request store `{ user, reqId }` — lets deep-stack code read the current user without prop-drilling |
66	| `helpers/openrouter.js` | OpenRouter API client; prompt-caching markers for anthropic/deepseek/google/qwen models |
67	| `helpers/deepseek.js` | DeepSeek direct API client (`api.deepseek.com`); maps `reasoning_effort: 'none'\|'high'\|'max'` to the DeepSeek `thinking` parameter; used when `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` is set and `api='deepseek-direct'` |
68	| `helpers/qwen.js` | Alibaba Qwen direct API client; Responses API session cache for `session_id` calls, Chat Completions explicit cache fallback for one-off calls; used when `DASHSCOPE_API_KEY` or `QWEN_API_KEY` is set and `api='qwen-direct'` |
69	| `helpers/cache-metrics.js` | Normalizes provider usage/cache telemetry into shared hit/miss/write/hit-rate fields |
70	| `services/ai-cache-telemetry.service.js` | Persists per-response AI cache telemetry into `ai_cache_events` and aggregates admin cache stats by provider/model/strategy |
71	| `helpers/section301.js` | Section 301 PDF text extraction + regex HTS parser (lazy-requires `pdf-parse`) |
72	| `helpers/hts-rates.js` | MFN base-rate lookup against bundled USITC HTS JSON (`data/hts-rates.json`) |
73	| `helpers/trump2025.js` | Trump 2025 reciprocal-tariff lookup; alias-aware country normalization |
74	| `helpers/factories.js` | Factory list / country map / `updateCountry(id, country)` |
75	| `helpers/digitalocean.js` | S3 client; presigned URL, ACL, upload, delete; `uploadBufferToSpaces(buffer, fileName, contentType)` for in-memory Buffer uploads (used by bulk import to avoid writing temp files) |
76	| `helpers/mention.js` | `parseMentions` / `resolveMentions` for @mention pipeline |
77	| `helpers/multer.js` | Multer instances: `multerGetThumb` (disk, thumbnails), `multerDigitalOcean` (disk, general uploads), `multerMemory` (memory storage, 25 MB limit — xlsx bulk imports) |
78	| `helpers/errorHandler.js` | Express error-handler middleware |
79	| `helpers/quote-access.js` | `vendorMayModifyQuote(userInfo, rfqVendor)` — ownership guard preventing a vendor from mutating another factory's quote by `quote_id` (see §11) |
80	| `helpers/rfqBulkImport.js` | Factory bulk-offer xlsx workflow: `generateTemplate(vendorName, customers)` → ExcelJS workbook buffer; `parseImport(buffer)` → `{rows, images}`; `getNextFoffPriceCodes(sql, count, Op)` → sequential `foff-XXXX` price code array |
81	| `helpers/rfqCopyMerchGroupGuard.js` | Guard: prevents copying an RFQ item into a closed/inactive merch group (uses `is_active` flag) |
82	| `helpers/rfqHistory.js` | Copy-lineage traversal via `rfqItem_copied_from_id`; used by `getRfqItemHistory` endpoint |
83	| `helpers/rfqStepDedupe.js` | Deduplication helper for RFQ step-change records |
84	| `helpers/utility.js` | Shared utility functions |
85	| `models/db.js` | Sequelize init + model registration + **all schema migrations** (`ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`) |
86	| `models/db/` | 60+ Sequelize model files (one per table) |
87	| `models/lib.model.js` | Legacy domain-method module (`Customer` namespace — see §11) |
88	| `routes/index.js` | Root router — mounts all subrouters |
89	| `routes/lib.router.js` | **150+ endpoints** mounted at `/` — RFQ, vendor, product CRUD. Never read whole — grep or use offset/limit |
90	| `routes/admin.router.js` | `/api/admin` — app settings, section 301, HTS rates, Trump 2025, factories, impersonation |
91	| `routes/auth.router.js` | `/auth` — Azure SSO login |
92	| `routes/notification.router.js` | `/api/notifications` |
93	| `routes/user.router.js` | `/api/user` — profile, photo upload |
94	| `routes/openrouter.router.js` | `/api/openrouter` — AI proxy |
95	| `routes/cross.router.js` | `/api/cross` — CBP CROSS rulings proxy |
96	| `routes/comment.router.js` | `/api/comment` |
97	| `routes/customer.router.js` | `/api/customers` |
98	| `routes/grid.router.js` | `/api/grid` |
99	| `routes/health.router.js` | `/api/health` |
100	| `routes/digitalocean.router.js` | File storage endpoints mounted at `/` |
101	| `routes/` (others) | `itemSize`, `itemDepth`, `std`, `role`, `autofill` |
102	| `services/` | Business logic layer — Sequelize queries and domain operations |
103	| `controllers/` | Thin route handlers; delegate to services or `models/lib.model.js` |
104	| `tests/unit/` | Jest unit tests (227 tests across 19 suites, hermetic, runs in ~1 s) |
105	| `data/hts-rates.json` | Bundled USITC HTS schedule (~1 MB, ~13.6 k codes) — regenerated yearly by `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` |
106	| `scripts/fetch-hts-rates.js` | One-shot downloader to rebuild `data/hts-rates.json` from USITC |
107	| `docs/` | `architecture.md`, `configuration.md`, `development.md`, `deployment.md` |
108	| `HTS_RAG.md` | Comprehensive implementation plan for the future internal HTS RAG/precedent system; includes mandatory shadow-mode validation against legacy CROSS before any fast path |
109	| `.env.example` | Template for local `.env.<env-name>` files |
110	| `Dockerfile` | Single-stage Node production image |
111	| `cloudbuild.yaml` | Google Cloud Build CI/CD definition |
112	| `node_modules/` | Third-party packages — **never edit** |
113	
114	---
115	
116	## 4. The Prime Directive
117	
118	The entire repo is custom code — there is no committed vendor framework directory analogous to the frontend's `src/@vex/`. Everything under the repo root is fair game **except**:
119	
120	- `node_modules/` — never edit.
121	- `models/db/*.js` model files are effectively schema declarations. When you add a column to a model, you **must** also add the matching `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` migration in `models/db.js` or the column will never exist in production. See §6 Decision Tree.
122	- `routes/lib.router.js` and `models/lib.model.js` are giant legacy files (150+ endpoints, ~3000 lines). **Add to them surgically rather than refactoring.** Any refactor that touches most of the file needs a coordinated effort — not a solo AI session.
123	
124	---
125	
126	## 5. Core modification inventory
127	
128	No third-party or framework files outside our directories have been intentionally modified. There is no vendor layer to track here.
129	
130	| File | Change made | Why | Risk |
131	|---|---|---|---|
132	| — | — | — | — |
133	
134	---
135	
136	## 6. Decision tree
137	
138	**Add a new API endpoint:**
139	1. Choose the right router (`lib.router.js` for RFQ/product/vendor; a named router for anything else)
140	2. Add a thin handler in `controllers/`; keep business logic in `services/` or `models/lib.model.js`
141	3. Gate with `authRole([...roles])` from `helpers/auth.js`
142	4. Add a Jest test in `tests/unit/`
143	5. Document in `docs/architecture.md` § Routes
144	
145	**Add a column to a database table:**
146	1. Add the field to the Sequelize model in `models/db/<Model>.js`
147	2. Add `sequelize.query(\`ALTER TABLE ${schema}"TableName" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "col_name" TYPE\`).catch(...)` inside `initialize()` in `models/db.js`. **If the column name is mixed-case (e.g. `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`), it MUST be double-quoted in the ALTER TABLE.** Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so an unquoted mixed-case name is folded to lowercase by Postgres and Sequelize never finds it — `findAll` then throws and the grid breaks (see §14, SYS-002).
148	3. Add a backfill query in the same block if needed (idempotent — guard with `WHERE col IS NULL`)
149	4. Add a regression assertion in `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`
150	5. **Do not skip step 2.** `sequelize.sync()` does NOT alter existing tables. Missing this step causes "column does not exist" crashes in production (see §14, SYS-030).

1	# AGENTS.md — designflow-tracking

4	Read this first; it should give you a working mental model in under 5 minutes.
5	
6	## 1. Project summary
7	
8	`designflow-tracking` is a Node/Express + Sequelize + PostgreSQL backend service in the
9	Designflow PLM platform. It serves the **tracking** domain used by the Angular frontend
10	(`designflow-frontend`) via the BFF: production tracking, licensing tracking, lead-time
11	calculation, and — added in this work — **sample tracking** (physical product samples moving
12	between factories and the Ningbo/NYC offices). The service is private (Cloud Run
13	`--no-allow-unauthenticated`); all browser traffic reaches it through the BFF at
14	`/api/tracking/*`.
15	
16	## Multi-model AI note
17	
18	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
19	
20	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
21	
22	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.

55	
56	| Path | What it is | Ownership |
57	|---|---|---|

62	| `models/db.js` | Sequelize bootstrap + idempotent migration block | code we own |

98	5. Only then add or change code.

1	# HANDOFF — Remaining open items
2	
3	Open items that need continuation context are listed below.

5	---
6	
7	## Performance follow-ups — **DEFERRED, earmarked for `albert-2sandbox` branch** (added 2026-06-22)
8	
9	These came out of the 2026-06-22 page-load performance review. The three lower-risk
10	items (RFQ init `forkJoin`, reference-data caching, per-row-expansion caching) were
11	**already implemented** on the working branch — see `docs/performance-caching.md`.
12	The items below were intentionally **not** done yet; do them on `albert-2sandbox`.
13	
14	### A. Server-side pagination for the RFQ `getAll` query — **DESIGNED (2026-06-23), pending developer review**
15	
16	> 📄 **Full implementation design: [`docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md`](docs/rfq-ssrm-migration-plan.md)** —
17	> a complete cross-repo (backend + frontend) SSRM-with-server-side-grouping plan with the field
18	> map, filter/sort translators, Res + merch-group filterability, group/leaf query design,
19	> full-dataset export, file list, test plan, risks, and open questions for the reviewer.
20	> Product-owner decisions baked in: keep server-side grouping; Res + merch-groups must be
21	> filterable (carton dims need not be); export the full filtered set. **Not yet implemented** —
22	> review the doc before building; ship behind the dual-endpoint so `/getAllItem` stays as rollback.
23	
24	**Problem:** the RFQ grid's backend `getAll` (`designflow-backend/models/lib.model.js`,
25	`Customer.getAll`) does an unbounded `findAll` across ~10 joined tables and returns
26	**all** rows (currently 3000+). It's the heaviest query behind the page.
27	
28	**⚠️ The critical caveat (why this is deferred):** the RFQ grid currently uses AG
29	Grid's **client-side row model** — all rows are in the browser, so filtering and
30	sorting work across the *entire* dataset for free. If you naïvely add `LIMIT/OFFSET`
31	to the backend **without** also moving to AG Grid's **server-side row model**, then
32	filter/sort will only act on the one page currently loaded — i.e. **filtering would
33	silently stop working across all 3000+ rows.** That is a functional regression, not
34	just a perf change.
35	
36	**Correct approach (the whole job):**
37	1. Switch the RFQ grid to the server-side row model (SSRM) — see how
38	   `itemLibrary` / `prod_tracking` already do this (they use SSRM today).
39	2. Push **filtering, sorting, and pagination to the backend** so they operate over
40	   the full set, and have `getAll` accept `startRow/endRow/filterModel/sortModel`.
41	3. Return a total row count for the grid.
42	4. Re-verify: Saved Views, the Division logic, default Step filter, master/detail
43	   expansion, export (must export the full filtered set, not just the loaded page),
44	   and cell notes.
45	
46	**Do not ship a bare `LIMIT` on `getAll`.** Either do the full SSRM migration or leave it.
47	
48	### B. Adopt `ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush` + precompute template getters
49	
50	**Problem:** only ~8 of ~278 components use OnPush. Grid pages (rfq, itemLibrary,
51	prod_tracking, etc.) use default change detection with method/getter bindings that
52	re-run every CD cycle (e.g. `valueGetter` doing `allMerchGroups.find(...)` per cell,
53	template getters that rebuild arrays).
54	
55	**Approach (incremental, per-component — do NOT flip globally):**
56	- Convert one heavy component at a time to `OnPush`; drive updates through the
57	  already-injected `ChangeDetectorRef` (`markForCheck()`), and use immutable updates
58	  (new array/object references) instead of in-place mutation.
59	- Precompute template getter results into fields when the source data changes.
60	- **Risk:** OnPush changes *when* the view updates — anything relying on mutate-in-place
61	  will silently stop refreshing. Each component needs manual testing after conversion.
62	
63	### C. N+1 / row-by-row writes → `bulkCreate` — **AUDIT-FIRST process defined (2026-06-23)**
64	
65	> 📄 **Audit template + staged process + hotspot inventory:
66	> [`designflow-data-syncing/docs/bulkcreate-migration-audit.md`](../designflow-data-syncing/docs/bulkcreate-migration-audit.md)**
67	> (lives in the repo where the work happens). It defines a per-hotspot fill-in audit checklist and a
68	> staged process: **(0) fix error visibility → (1) per-hotspot audit incl. the conditional-update
69	> decision → (2) verify/add the DB unique constraint → (3) baseline + tests + scheduler validation
70	> in non-prod → (4) chunked `bulkCreate` in a transaction → (5) parity check → next service.**
71	> Migrate **one service at a time**; first service is a pattern-setter (merch-group sync), NOT the
72	> riskiest (`getProdOrderDetail`).
73	
74	This is primarily a **backend / sync-service** concern, listed here so the frontend
75	handoff points at it. Row-by-row `INSERT`/`UPDATE` loops (often with a per-row
76	`SELECT` first) should become batched `bulkCreate(rows, { updateOnDuplicate: [...] })`
77	or batched `findAll({ where: { [Op.in]: keys } })`. Hotspots identified:
78	`designflow-data-syncing` sync loops (e.g. `getProdOrderDetail`, merch-group sync,
79	prepack associations) and `designflow-tracking` `Box.addSamples`.
80	
81	**Why "investigate further", not "just do it":**
82	- Each site needs a real **unique constraint** + an explicit `updateOnDuplicate`
83	  column list — wrong list either clobbers kept data or silently skips updates.
84	- Some loops do **conditional** updates ("only if incoming `modTime` is newer") that
85	  `updateOnDuplicate` can't express without a SQL `CASE`/literal — naive batching can
86	  resurrect stale data.
87	- Per-row **hooks/side-effects** (Teams notifications, audit writes) are skipped by
88	  `bulkCreate` unless `individualHooks: true` (which removes most of the speed gain).
89	- Watch Postgres bound-parameter limits (~65k) → cap batch size; wrap multi-step
90	  writes in a transaction for atomicity.
91	
92	Action: audit each hotspot for the four points above, then migrate service-by-service
93	with the upsert keys verified against existing data first.
94	
95	---

98

130	## Factory bulk offer upload — **IMPLEMENTED, NOT YET COMMITTED** (updated 2026-06-11)
131	
132	A vendor-only "Upload Offers" feature was fully implemented across both `designflow-frontend` and `designflow-backend` in this session. All code sits as unstaged changes on branch `sandbox-albert` in both repos.
133	
134	### What it does
135	
136	Factory/vendor users see an **Upload Offers** button (`@if (role === 'vendor')`) in the RFQ toolbar. Clicking it opens a 3-stage dialog:
137	
138	1. **Form** — download a pre-formatted `.xlsx` template (pre-filled with factory name, date, Customer dropdown, Price Terms dropdown, 50 tall data rows with yellow image cells); name the RFQ group; drop-zone to select the completed file
139	2. **Uploading** — spinner while the API processes the file
140	3. **Result** — list of created items (price code + description) and any row-level errors (bilingual English · Chinese)
141	
142	On the backend, the import creates:
143	- One `RFQGroup` (named by the factory)
144	- One `RFQItem` per non-empty data row — price code `foff-XXXX` (global sequential; padded to 4 digits)
145	- One `RFQVendor` quote record per row (linked to the uploading factory's `factory_id` from JWT; `req_status = 2` = quoted; lands at @factories step)
146	- Embedded product images extracted from the xlsx and uploaded to DigitalOcean Spaces via `uploadBufferToSpaces`

268	**Status:** shared-key verification **DONE (GO, 99.3%)**; Phase-0 bridge **designed, not yet built**.
269	
270	**Context:** See `docs/licensing-tracking-poppim-integration.md` (analysis + verified result) and
271	`docs/licensing-poppim-phase0-bridge.md` (the build spec). Albert's direction (2026-06-13): DesignFlow
272	stays the system of record for licensing approval; poppim (Directus app at `pm.designflow.app`) consumes a
273	**read-only** copy.
274	
275	**Done (this session):**
276	- Confirmed both data models; the two run on separate clouds/DBs (API↔API, no shared DB).
277	- **Verified the join key (GO):** DesignFlow `item_num_id` ⇄ the item-number prefix of poppim
278	  `product.name` (NOT `product.code`). **9,224 / 9,293 (99.3%)** poppim item-nos match DesignFlow; misses
279	  are truncated codes. Matches are almost entirely licensed (div 1/2/8). Key is **not** unique on
280	  `item_num_id` alone → use `(item_num_id, compan_code_fk, div_code_fk)`.
281	- Wrote the Phase-0 bridge spec (read-only, one-way DesignFlow→poppim status mirror).
282	
283	**Next action (build the bridge — see `docs/licensing-poppim-phase0-bridge.md`):**
284	1. Collect the 4 open inputs (spec §8): export the poppim `stage` collection; run
285	   `SELECT id, phase, status, "order" FROM designflow."LicenseFeedBacks" ORDER BY "order";`; obtain a
286	   Directus service-account write token; pick hosting (Cloud Run job vs Directus Flow).
287	2. Add the read-only `df_*` fields + backfill `product_link.linked_external_id` in the `u2giants/directus`
288	   schema repo.
289	3. Build the bulk read endpoint on DesignFlow (`/lic/status-export`) and the idempotent sync job.
290	4. Dry-run/log-only one cycle, spot-check 20 products, then enable writes.
291	
292	**Risks / watchouts:**
293	- Key needs the `(item_num_id + company + division)` composite (`item_num_id` alone has ~1,500 DF
294	  collisions; 367 poppim products share an item-no). `compan_code_fk` is often NULL — tolerate it.
295	- ~42% of poppim "products" are non-product ClickUp rows (comments / routing notes / presentations) — the
296	  bridge must filter these, not treat every poppim product row as a SKU.
297	- The bridge is **API↔API across GCP↔Coolify**; there is no shared DB. Do not assume a SQL join is possible.

5	Node.js / Express HTTP API (CommonJS). Stateless — identity comes from the JWT on every request. PostgreSQL is the only persistent store; DigitalOcean Spaces holds uploaded files. No in-memory session state.
6	
7	```
8	Browser / Frontend SPA
9	  └── BFF (designflow-bff, port 5004)
10	        └── Core backend (this repo, port 5000)
11	              ├── PostgreSQL (Sequelize ORM)
12	              └── DigitalOcean Spaces (S3-compatible file storage)
13	
14	External outbound calls:
15	  POST https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/...    ← AI classification proxy
16	  GET  https://rulings.cbp.gov/api/search  ← CBP CROSS rulings proxy
17	  POST https://api.brevo.com/v3/...        ← transactional email
18	  GET  https://login.microsoftonline.com/  ← Azure JWKS (key cache 10 min)
19	```
20	
21	---
22	
23	## Project layout
24	
25	```
26	index.js                    Server entry: middleware wiring, listen
27	config/
28	  env.js                    Resolves .env.${NODE_ENV} filename, loads dotenv
29	  db.config.js              PostgreSQL connection params (throws on missing key)
30	  digitalocean.config.js    DO Spaces credentials from env
31	  brevo.config.js           Brevo email credentials from env
32	helpers/
33	  auth.js                   JWT RS256 verify; authRole(roles[]) middleware factory; getUserInfo(token)
34	  context.middleware.js     AsyncLocalStorage per-request context: { user, reqId }
35	  digitalocean.js           S3 client; getPreSignedUrl, setObjectPublic, uploadObjectsToBucket, deleteObjectsFromBucket, setCORSForBucket
36	  openrouter.js             OpenRouter API client: listUserModels, chatCompletion (with prompt-caching markers)
37	  cross.js                  CBP CROSS rulings proxy
38	  section301.js             Section 301 PDF text extraction + regex HTS parser (lazy-requires pdf-parse)
39	  hts-rates.js              MFN base-rate lookup against bundled USITC HTS JSON (data/hts-rates.json)
40	  trump2025.js              Trump 2025 reciprocal-tariff country-rate lookup (alias-aware)
41	  factories.js              Factory list / country map / update-country accessors
42	  mention.js                parseMentions(text) → @handles; resolveMentions(handles, users) → IDs
43	  multer.js                 Multer instances (memory storage) configured per upload type
44	  imageMagick.service.js    ImageMagick shell wrapper for image processing
45	  jimp.service.js           Jimp-based image resize/thumbnail
46	  brevo.js                  Brevo email send wrapper
47	  utility.js                Shared utility functions
48	  errorHandler.js           Express error-handler middleware
49	data/
50	  hts-rates.json            ~1 MB compact USITC HTS export: { "NNNN.NN.NN": { d, g } } (13.6k codes)
51	scripts/
52	  fetch-hts-rates.js        One-shot downloader that rebuilds data/hts-rates.json from
53	                            https://hts.usitc.gov/reststop/exportList — re-run yearly when
54	                            USITC publishes a new revision
55	models/
56	  db.js                     Sequelize init + model registration + safe migrations (ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS;
57	                            mixed-case identifiers MUST be double-quoted — see Inline migrations below)
58	  db/                       60+ Sequelize model files
59	routes/
60	  index.js                  Root router — mounts all subrouters (see Routes below)
61	  lib.router.js             Mounted at /  — RFQ, product, vendor CRUD (150+ endpoints)
62	  digitalocean.router.js    Mounted at /  — file storage endpoints
63	  auth.router.js            Mounted at /auth
64	  admin.router.js           Mounted at /api/admin
65	  notification.router.js    Mounted at /api/notifications
66	  user.router.js            Mounted at /api/user
67	  openrouter.router.js      Mounted at /api/openrouter
68	  cross.router.js           Mounted at /api/cross
69	  comment.router.js         Mounted at /api/comment
70	  customer.router.js        Mounted at /api/customers
71	  grid.router.js            Mounted at /api/grid
72	  health.router.js          Mounted at /api
73	  ... (itemSize, itemDepth, std, role, autofill)
74	services/                   Business logic layer (Sequelize queries, domain operations)
75	controllers/                Route handler functions (thin — delegate to services)
76	tests/unit/                 Jest unit tests
77	```
78	
79	---
80	
81	## Routes
82	
83	### Health
84	- `GET /api/health` → `{ status: 'ok', message, timestamp }`
85	
86	### Authentication
87	- `POST /auth/azure` — exchange Azure AD ID token for Designflow JWT; auto-provisions new users (unauthenticated)
88	
89	### User
90	- `GET  /api/user/me` — current user profile `{ firstName, name, email, photoUrl }` (all roles). Source table depends on the JWT `role`: staff resolve from `users` by `id`; `vendor` (factory) users resolve from the `vendor` table by `vendor_id` (see AGENTS.md §11). Returns 404 only if no matching row exists.
91	- `POST /api/user/photo` — upload profile avatar (multipart `photo`, max 5 MB, all roles). Stored on DO Spaces under `profile-photos/user-<id>` or `profile-photos/vendor-<id>` by role; URL persisted to `users.profile_photo` or `vendor.vendor_profile_photo`.
92	
93	### Notifications
94	- `GET  /api/notifications` — unread notifications for current user (most recent 20, all roles)
95	- `GET  /api/notifications/count` — unread count for current user (all roles)
96	- `GET  /api/notifications/mentionable-users` — active users for @mention autocomplete (all roles)
97	- `POST /api/notifications/create` — create notification for a specific user (all roles)
98	- `PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read` — mark one notification read (all roles)
99	- `POST /api/notifications/read-all` — mark all notifications read (all roles)
100	- `DELETE /api/notifications` — permanently delete all notifications for current user (all roles)
101	
102	### Admin
103	- `GET  /api/admin/app-settings/:key` — read setting (admin)
104	- `PUT  /api/admin/app-settings/:key` — write setting (admin)
105	- `POST /api/admin/section301/upload` — parse USTR Section 301 PDF; store HTS lookup (admin). Legacy — UI-hidden but still wired.
106	- `POST /api/admin/section301/upload-csv` — parse USTR Section 301 CSV (preferred over PDF); same `app_settings.section301_lookup` storage (admin)
107	- `GET  /api/admin/section301/status` — `{ loaded, codesFound, lists }` — no full payload (admin, sourcing_manager)
108	- `GET  /api/admin/section301/lookup?hts=` — look up one HTS code (admin, sourcing_manager)
109	- `GET  /api/admin/hts-rates/status` — `{ loaded, codesFound }` for the bundled USITC schedule (admin, sourcing_manager, designer)
110	- `GET  /api/admin/hts-rates/lookup?hts=` — MFN general rate by HTS code (10→8→6-digit prefix fallback)
111	- `GET  /api/admin/trump2025/list` — Trump 2025 country → rate list (admin, sourcing_manager, designer)
112	- `PUT  /api/admin/trump2025/list` — replace the full list (admin only)
113	- `GET  /api/admin/trump2025/lookup?country=` — alias-aware case-insensitive rate lookup
114	- `GET  /api/admin/factories` — full factory list with `factory_country` (admin, sourcing_manager)
115	- `GET  /api/admin/factories/countries` — compact `{ factory_nickname: factory_country }` map (all RFQ-viewing roles)
116	- `PATCH /api/admin/factories/:id` — update one factory's `factory_country` (admin, sourcing_manager)
117	- `POST /api/admin/impersonate/:userId` — issue 2-hour impersonation JWT for a normal user, `{ targetType: 'factory' }` visible factory row, or `{ targetType: 'vendor' }` factory rep (admin)
118	- Art types, artist types, artists, age groups, merch groups CRUD
119	
120	### AI / OpenRouter proxy
121	- `GET  /api/openrouter/models` — proxies GET /api/v1/models/user from openrouter.ai; requires Designflow JWT via `authRole(['designer', 'sourcing_manager', 'vendor', 'sales', 'production'])`
122	- `POST /api/openrouter/chat` — proxies chat completions; requires the same route auth as `/models`
123	
124	### CBP CROSS
125	- `GET  /api/cross/search?term=&pageSize=10&page=1` — proxies CBP CROSS ruling search (no auth)
126	
127	### File storage (mounted at root `/`)
128	- `POST /getPreSignedUrl` — generate S3 presigned PUT URL for direct browser upload (15 min expiry)
129	- `POST /setObjectPublic` — apply `public-read` ACL to an uploaded object; returns `{ publicUrl }` — called by frontend after upload to make the object publicly accessible
130	- `POST /saveTechLink/:id` — save a DO Spaces file URL to an item record
131	- `POST /deleteBucketObjects` — delete files from the bucket
132	- `GET  /getListOfBuckets`, `POST /getBucketObjects`, `PUT /setCORSForBucket` — bucket admin
133	
134	### RFQ / product grid
135	- `POST /api/grid/getAllItem`, `getGridHeader`, `saveGridLayout`, `getCustomGridLayout`
136	- Vendor quotes (in `routes/lib.router.js`): `POST /updateVendor`, `/sendPriceReq`, `/addingVendor`. **Re-quote (stale price refresh):** `POST /requestRequote` (internal roles only — flags a factory's quote and emails the factory for a fresh price) and `POST /keepPrice` (vendor reconfirms the current price, clearing the flag). Both resolve the quote by `quote_id` (`RFQVendor_id`) and run through the `vendorMayModifyQuote` ownership guard. See `models/lib.model.js` `Customer.requestRequote` / `Customer.keepPrice`.
137	- **Archive:** `POST /archiveRFQItem` archives/un-archives an item. For internal roles it toggles the global `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive`; for vendors it toggles a **per-vendor** `RFQVendor_archived` on their own quote row (so it never hides the item from Sourcing or other factories). On a vendor's list load, `Customer.getAll` auto-archives that factory's priced quotes older than 3 months (`helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js`). A vendor un-archive is sticky (`RFQVendor_archive_optout`); `requestRequote`, a fresh price, and `keepPrice` auto-un-archive / reset the cycle. See AGENTS.md §11.
138	
139	### Selected Vendor Persistence
140	
141	What changed:
142	`POST /selectedVendor` maps the RFQ grid payload through `utility.mapRFQItem()` and updates the matching `RFQItem` row. It looks up by `rfqItem_id` when present and falls back to `rfqItem_style_number` for older callers.
143	
144	Why:
145	The selected factory is stored on `RFQItem.rfqItem_choosen_vendor`; writing this payload to `GridLayout` targets the wrong table and does not persist the item choice.
146	
147	Future sessions should:
148	Keep selected-vendor changes on `RFQItem`, not `GridLayout`, and run `tests/unit/selected-vendor.model.test.js` when changing `/selectedVendor` or RFQ chosen-vendor mapping.
149	
150	### Other
151	- `/api/customers/**`, `/api/item-size/**`, `/api/item_depth/**`, `/api/comment/**`, `/api/role/**`, `/api/autofill/**`, `/std/**`
152	
153	---
154	
155	## Authentication
156	
157	All protected routes use `authRole(roles[])` from `helpers/auth.js`:
158	
159	1. Reads `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. For Cloud Run requests coming through the BFF, `index.js` first middleware swaps `x-user-authorization` → `authorization` (the BFF puts the OIDC token in `authorization` for Cloud Run; the user JWT travels in `x-user-authorization` and is restored before any route handler runs)
160	2. `jwt.verify(token, JWT_PUBLIC_KEY, { algorithms: ['RS256'] })`
161	3. Checks `decoded.role` against the allowed roles array
162	4. Returns 403 if token missing/invalid; 401 if role not in allowed list
163	
164	`contextMiddleware` runs on every request (before auth) and populates `AsyncLocalStorage` with `{ user: decoded|null, reqId: uuid4 }` via `getUserInfo(token)`, which always resolves and never throws.
165	
166	**Role values:** `admin`, `sales`, `designer`, `production`, `sourcing_manager`, `vendor`
167	
168	---
169	
170	## Azure SSO login pipeline
171	
172	`POST /auth/azure` accepts `{ idToken }` (Azure AD ID token), returns `{ status: 'success', token }` (Designflow RS256 JWT, 12 h TTL).
173	
174	1. `verifyAzureToken(idToken)` — decode token header, fetch matching public key from Azure JWKS at `login.microsoftonline.com/${TENANT_ID}/discovery/v2.0/keys` (cached 10 min), verify signature + audience + issuer
175	2. `findOrCreateUser(azurePayload)` — look up by email (`preferred_username` or `email` claim) in `users` then `vendor` table. If not found, auto-provision `users` row with `level: 'designer'`; admin promotes from there
176	3. Sign Designflow JWT: `{ name, user_id, email, role }`, RS256, 12 h
177	
178	**Intentional:** `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` / `AZURE_TENANT_ID` are read at module load time — missing values cause `jwt.verify` to throw with audience/issuer error → 401 `'Authentication failed'`.
179	
180	---
181	
182	## Section 301 tariff pipeline
183	
184	Uploaded once by admin; stored permanently in `app_settings.section301_lookup`. Re-upload only when USTR revises the tariff lists.
185	
186	**CSV upload (`POST /api/admin/section301/upload-csv`, preferred):**
187	1. Multer reads CSV into memory (10 MB limit, no disk writes); admin uploads a USTR all-lists export with `HTS,Description,List` columns.
188	2. `helpers/section301-csv.js` parses with quote-aware char-by-char splitting (the source CSV has multi-line quoted descriptions and embedded commas).
189	3. The file's repeated preamble + header blocks (~every 25 data rows in the real export) are skipped; only data rows are kept.
190	4. Three HTS code shapes accepted: `NNNN.NN.NN`, `NNNN.NN.NN.NN`, `NNNN.NN.NNNN`. Internal whitespace stripped from the HTS column to recover one known typo (`8471. 70.40`).
191	5. Rate is statutory per list (List 1/2/3 → 25%, List 4A → 7.5%, List 4B → 0% labeled "List 4B (suspended)").
192	6. Result stored as JSON in `app_settings['section301_lookup']` — same key/shape as the legacy PDF parser, so the rest of the system is unchanged.
193	
194	The response payload includes `{ codesFound, lists, skipped, conflicts, lineErrors }` so the admin UI can show a verification summary.
195	
196	**Why CSV replaced PDF:** the original PDF path used regex section-boundary detection on `pdf-parse` text output. When `pdf-parse` interleaved pages (multi-column layouts, embedded images), codes near a List 3 marker could be wrongly assigned to that list when they actually belonged to List 4A. A real-world example (`4414.00.00`, picture frames — actually List 4A 7.5%) was being reported as List 3 25%. CSV input is column-addressed: row 5 column C IS the list, no inference. The PDF endpoint still exists at `POST /api/admin/section301/upload` but the admin UI no longer exposes it.
197	
198	**Legacy PDF upload (`POST /api/admin/section301/upload`, still wired but UI-hidden):**
199	- `pdf-parse` extracts text; regex detects `List 1/2/3/4A` or `Annex A/B/C/E` markers; `\b(\d{4}\.\d{2}\.(?:\d{4}|\d{2}))\b` extracts codes. Rates hardcoded per list. `pdf-parse` is lazy-required so a broken/missing package doesn't crash startup.

Based on my comprehensive search across all 6 repos under "C:\repos\dflow plm", I have found no documentation that explicitly explains why an aws-sdk file was deleted or removed. Here's what I discovered:

## Summary of Findings

**No evidence of aws-sdk deletion or removal found in documentation.**

The search across all 6 repositories (designflow-backend, designflow-bff, designflow-data-syncing, designflow-frontend, designflow-item-master, designflow-tracking) yielded:

1. **Current aws-sdk usage is alive and well:**
   - `designflow-backend/helpers/digitalocean.js` actively uses `@aws-sdk/client-s3` and `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner`
   - `designflow-tracking/helpers/digitalocean.js` contains similar aws-sdk dependencies
   - `designflow-backend/package.json` lists: `"@aws-sdk/client-s3": "^3.990.0"` and `"@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner": "^3.990.0"`

2. **DigitalOcean Spaces (S3-compatible) is the current storage solution:**
   - Architecture docs consistently reference DigitalOcean Spaces for file storage
   - `designflow-backend/docs/architecture.md` (line 12): "DigitalOcean Spaces (S3-compatible file storage)"
   - `designflow-backend/AGENTS.md` (line 75): "S3 client; presigned URL, ACL, upload, delete; `uploadBufferToSpaces`"
   - README.md (line 14): "DigitalOcean Spaces (S3-compatible) — file storage for item images and customer logos"

3. **No removal documentation found in:**
   - CLAUDE.md files (5 files checked)
   - AGENTS.md files (checked backend and tracking)
   - HANDOFF.md files (5 files checked)
   - README.md files
   - All `/docs/` directories across all 6 repos

**Conclusion:** There is no documentation explaining why an aws-sdk file was deleted or removed, because currently the aws-sdk packages are not deleted — they remain active dependencies used for S3-compatible DigitalOcean Spaces operations. If there was a historical aws-sdk file removal, it is not documented in the current markdown files, HANDOFF notes, or architectural documentation.
